JUDICIAL DEPARTMENT Writ Petition No.45094 of 2021 Muhammad Khalid Mushtaq Versus The Board of Revenue, Government of Punjab etc. ================================================= JUDGMENT Date of Hearing: 08.12.2021 & 09.12.2021 Petitioner by:- Mian Irfan Akram, Advocate. Mr. Shahzad Mehmood Butt, Advocate for the petitioner in Writ Petition No.62184/2021. Respondents by:- Mr. Muhammad Akram Khurram, AAG M/s Farooq Amjad Mir, Ahmad Farooq Mir, Muhammad Rafique Shad, Advocates for respondent No.5. Mr. Waseem Iqbal Butt, Advocate for Chief Settlement Commissioner with Record. CH. MUHAMMAD IQBAL, J:- Through this single judgment, I intend to decide the titled Writ Petition along with connected Writ Petition No.62184/2021, as common questions of law and facts are involved in both these petitions. 2. Through these writ petitions under Article 199 of the Constitution of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan, 1973, the petitioners have challenged the validity of order 02.06.2021 passed by the Member (J-V) / Incharge Settlement Wing / Administrator (Residual Properties) / Notified Officer, Board of Revenue, Punjab who validated the transfer of Writ Petition No.45094/2021 2 Writ Petition No.62184/2021 land measuring 13-Kanals 10-Marlas comprising Khasra Nos.229, 230 of Village Dhana Singhwala Lahore in favour of the PTCL/respondent No.5 and declared the PTO No.709071 and PTO No.709072, both dated 12.03.1962 purportedly issued in the name of Ghulam Muhammad and his son Muhammad Hussain as well as subsequent PTDs dated 21.03.1969 issued in the name of subsequent alleged transferee Abdul Ghani and Mst.

MUHAMMAD IQBAL, J:- Through this single judgment, I intend to decide the titled Writ Petition along with connected Writ Petition No.62184/2021, as common questions of law and facts are involved in both these petitions. 2. Through these writ petitions under Article 199 of the Constitution of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan, 1973, the petitioners have challenged the validity of order 02.06.2021 passed by the Member (J-V) / Incharge Settlement Wing / Administrator (Residual Properties) / Notified Officer, Board of Revenue, Punjab who validated the transfer of Writ Petition No.45094/2021 2 Writ Petition No.62184/2021 land measuring 13-Kanals 10-Marlas comprising Khasra Nos.229, 230 of Village Dhana Singhwala Lahore in favour of the PTCL/respondent No.5 and declared the PTO No.709071 and PTO No.709072, both dated 12.03.1962 purportedly issued in the name of Ghulam Muhammad and his son Muhammad Hussain as well as subsequent PTDs dated 21.03.1969 issued in the name of subsequent alleged transferee Abdul Ghani and Mst. Safia Latif etc., as bogus, forged and fabricated documents and also held that the mutation No.3227 dated 30.11.2005 was allegedly got sanctioned without prior permission / verification from the office of the (S&R) Wing of the Settlement Department. 3. Brief facts as contained in these petitions are that Pakistan Telephone & Telegraph Department (T&T), Government of Pakistan [which department was later on renamed as Pakistan Telecommunication Company Limited (PTCL)] intended to purchase the evacuee land for construction of its offices, telecommunication installations, a colony for its employees as well as a School, Maternity Home, Dispensary, Recreation Club and Playground at Dhana Singhwala near Punjab University, New Campus, Lahore and in this regard made a request to the Settlement Department through letter dated 04.06.1970. In response to the above request, it was decided on 06.06.1970 to alienate Writ Petition No.45094/2021 3 Writ Petition No.62184/2021 land measuring 1200-Kanals in favour of the then T&T Department against consideration of Rs.24,00,000/-. This consideration amount was paid to the Settlement Department through two cheques one amounting to Rs.8,00,000/- and other 16,00,000/-. This payment of consideration was duly acknowledged by the Transferring Department through letter dated 13.10.1970. The Central Government through the Ministry of Rehabilitation & Work, Islamabad accorded approval on 07.04.1971 and evacuee land measuring 800- Kanals was initially sold to respondent No.5/T&T Department (PTCL). 4. That after payment of all dues Transfer Order (TO) No.74635 dated 26.06.1979 was issued in favour of the then T&T Department for land measuring 738-Kanals 06-Marlas including Khasra Nos.229 & 230 of Moza Dhana Singhwala and mutation No.771 was sanctioned on 12.06.1982 in favour of T&T Department/PTCL.

The Central Government through the Ministry of Rehabilitation & Work, Islamabad accorded approval on 07.04.1971 and evacuee land measuring 800- Kanals was initially sold to respondent No.5/T&T Department (PTCL). 4. That after payment of all dues Transfer Order (TO) No.74635 dated 26.06.1979 was issued in favour of the then T&T Department for land measuring 738-Kanals 06-Marlas including Khasra Nos.229 & 230 of Moza Dhana Singhwala and mutation No.771 was sanctioned on 12.06.1982 in favour of T&T Department/PTCL. Further land measuring 28-Kanals was transferred through Transfer Order [TO No.0000563] dated 16.06.1993 which was duly verified by the Settlement Department/Board of Revenue on 10.07.1993. 5. Being dejected from sanctioning of the mutation in favour of respondent No.5 with regard to land comprising khasra No.229 (3k.16M) and Khasra No.230 (9k.14M) of Mauza Dhanna Singhwala, Muhammad Hussain son of Ghulam Muhammad etc. filed Writ Petition [No.167-R of Writ Petition No.45094/2021 4 Writ Petition No.62184/2021 1998] on 04.11.1998 claiming to be allottee of the aforesaid land (bearing Khasra Nos.229 & 230) through T.O No.709071 and 709072 dated 12.03.1962 as well as PTD Nos. 940 & 941 both dated 21.03.1969. The said writ petition was accepted by this Court vide judgment dated 19.07.1999. Against the said judgment Haroon Mahmood Masih etc. also filed petition under Section 12 C.P.C. [C.M.No.1/2000] claiming himself to be transferee from the allottee Muhammad Hussain s/o Ghulam Muhammad who hold PTDs No.154-CSC-4-5/4/193 dated 17.01.1962 and PTD No.125-CSC-4-5/4/196 dated 21.03.1962. The said application was accepted vide order dated 09.08.2001, the judgment dated 19.07.1999 was recalled and matter was remanded to the Notified Officer for decision of the matter as per law. 6. Against the said remand order, Abdul Ghani and Mst. Safia Latif filed petition under Section 12 C.P.C. [C.M. No.1720/2004] in CM No.1/2000 in W.P No.167-R.98. In the C.M. No.1720/2004 respondent No.5/ PTCL filed an application under Order I Rule 10 C.P.C [C.M. No.572/2006]. These applications were heard on 18.04.2006 and vide order announced on 04.08.2006 whereby petition under Section 12 C.P.C. [C.M.

No.572/2006]. These applications were heard on 18.04.2006 and vide order announced on 04.08.2006 whereby petition under Section 12 C.P.C. [C.M. No.1720/2004] was allowed to the extent of allotment of applicants (Abdul Ghani and Mst. Safia Latif) whereas the application under Order 1 Writ Petition No.45094/2021 5 Writ Petition No.62184/2021 Rule 10 C.P.C [C.M.No.572/2006] of PTCL/respondent No.5 was dismissed. 7. Feeling aggrieved of the order announced on 04.08.2006 [date of hearing dated 18.04.2006], PTCL through its GM filed Civil Petition No.1994-L/2006 and the Hon‟ble Supreme Court of Pakistan vide order dated 04.03.2009 converted civil petition into Civil Appeal [No.108-L/2009], accepted the same and remanded the case to the Chief Settlement Commissioner for decision. In aforesaid Civil Appeal, Abdul Ghani and Mst. Safia Latif filed C.M.A Nos.2207-L, 2208-L & 2209-L of 2009 which were dismissed by the Hon'ble Supreme Court of Pakistan vide order dated 21.06.2012 and maintained its remand order dated 04.03.2009 passed in Civil Appeal No.108-L/2009. 8. The pending proceeding in this Court i.e. F.A.O [No.204/2011 titled as PTCL etc. Vs. Khalid Mushtaq etc.], Writ Petition [No.53-R/2014 titled as PTCL Vs. Province of Punjab etc.] as well as application under Section 12 CPC (C.M.No.255-C/2011) in Civil Revision No.2858/1994 and petition under Section 12 C.P.C. (C.M. No.256-C) of 2011 in Civil Revision No.2857/1994 filed by respondent No.5- PTCL were disposed of vide order dated 18.05.2018 with the consent of the parties whereby this court also remanded the matter to the Chief Settlement Commissioner to decide the same in accordance with law. Writ Petition No.45094/2021 6 Writ Petition No.62184/2021 9. In the post remand proceedings the Chief Settlement Commissioner / Member (J-V) / Incharge Settlement Wing / Administrator (Residual Properties) / Notified Officer, Board of Revenue, Punjab vide order dated 02.06.2021 declared that the Provisional Transfer Orders Nos.709071 and 709072, both dated 12.03.1962 allegedly issued in the name of Ghulam Muhammad and his son Muhammad Hussain as well as subsequent Permanent Transfer Deeds (PTDs) dated 21.03.1969 in favour of Mst.

Writ Petition No.45094/2021 6 Writ Petition No.62184/2021 9. In the post remand proceedings the Chief Settlement Commissioner / Member (J-V) / Incharge Settlement Wing / Administrator (Residual Properties) / Notified Officer, Board of Revenue, Punjab vide order dated 02.06.2021 declared that the Provisional Transfer Orders Nos.709071 and 709072, both dated 12.03.1962 allegedly issued in the name of Ghulam Muhammad and his son Muhammad Hussain as well as subsequent Permanent Transfer Deeds (PTDs) dated 21.03.1969 in favour of Mst. Safia Latif and Abdul Ghani etc. as bogus, forged and fabricated documents as well as cancelled the mutation No.3227 dated 30.11.2005 being sanctioned without prior permission of the (S&R) Wing of the Chief Settlement Commissioner Office and validated the transfer of land measuring 13-Kanals 10-Marlas comprising Khasra Nos.229, 230 of Village Dhana Singhwala Lahore in favour of the PTCL. Hence, these writ petitions. 10. I have heard the learned counsels for the parties at full length and gone through the record with their able assistance. 11. As the main controversy revolves around the allotment of the evacuee land to Muhammad Hussain and Ghulam Muhammad as well as sale of the said evacuee land to respondent No.5. For resolving the controversy among the parties, following points are formulated that; Writ Petition No.45094/2021 7 Writ Petition No.62184/2021 1. Whether the respondent No.5/PTCL has valid title in its favour whereas PTO Nos.709071 & 709072 dated 12.03.1962 in favour of Muhammad Hussain and Ghulam Muhammad and PTDs dated 21.03.1969 in favour of Abdul Ghani and Mst. Safia Latif were not validly issued? 2. Whether evacuee land of Khasra Nos.229 & 230 of Mouza Dhana Singhwala falls within the category of building site or same was available for allotment against any evacuee claim? 3. Whether the Chief Settlement Commissioner / Notified Officer has the jurisdiction after promulgation of the Evacuee Property and Displaced Persons Laws (Repeal) Act, 1975 to decide the issue arising from allotment of the evacuee land? 4. Whether the subsequent claimants/purchasers from the original allottee have protection of bonafide purchaser under Section 41 of the Transfer of Property Act? 5.

Whether the Chief Settlement Commissioner / Notified Officer has the jurisdiction after promulgation of the Evacuee Property and Displaced Persons Laws (Repeal) Act, 1975 to decide the issue arising from allotment of the evacuee land? 4. Whether the subsequent claimants/purchasers from the original allottee have protection of bonafide purchaser under Section 41 of the Transfer of Property Act? 5. Whether the matter has already been finalized upto the Hon’ble Supreme Court of Pakistan which cannot be re-opened? 12. As the main dispute between the parties is regarding their claim to have a valid title of the land in question granted by the Settlement Department in their respective favour, as such, the Point No.1, formulated as above, is being decided at the first instance. Admittedly, the subject matter of this litigation is evacuee land comprising Khasra Nos.229 & 230 measuring 13-K 10-M of Mauza Dhana Singhwala. The Chief Settlement & Rehabilitation Commissioner, West Pakistan sold evacuee land measuring 800-Kanals situated in Mouza Dhana Singhwala, Tehsil Lahore Cantt., vide order dated 04.06.1970 in favour of respondent No.5 and subsequently quantum of said sold land was enhanced upto land measuring 1200-Kanals through letter dated 13.10.1970 and Writ Petition No.45094/2021 8 Writ Petition No.62184/2021 acknowledged the receipt of consideration amount of Rs.24,00,000/- through letter No.F-5 70-WI dated 07.04.1971 and above transaction was given effect in the Revenue Record and possession was delivered to PTCL vide Roznamcha Waqiati dated 24.08.1971 of Mouza Dhana Singhwala. One Muhammad Hussain filed a civil suit against the Board of Revenue etc. and got decree dated 29.07.1978 in respect of land measuring 1887-Kanals 14-Marlas of Mouza Dhana Singhwala, including the land transferred in favour of T&T Department (PTCL). The Province of Punjab challenged the above referred appellate and revisional orders [of this court] but remained unsuccessful. The aforesaid dicta of lower judicial fora was assailed through filing a civil petition and the Hon‟ble Supreme Court of Pakistan converted the said civil petition into appeal and accepted the same declaring the suit of Muhammad Hussain etc. as barred by limitation and set aside all the judgments of the lower courts as well as that of the High Court and dismissed the suit through judgment dated 10.02.1992 which is reported as [Province of the Punjab through Member Board of Revenue, (Residual Properties), Lahore & Others Vs Muhammad Hussain through Legal Heirs & Others (PLD 1993 SC 147)].

The Province of Punjab challenged the above referred appellate and revisional orders [of this court] but remained unsuccessful. The aforesaid dicta of lower judicial fora was assailed through filing a civil petition and the Hon‟ble Supreme Court of Pakistan converted the said civil petition into appeal and accepted the same declaring the suit of Muhammad Hussain etc. as barred by limitation and set aside all the judgments of the lower courts as well as that of the High Court and dismissed the suit through judgment dated 10.02.1992 which is reported as [Province of the Punjab through Member Board of Revenue, (Residual Properties), Lahore & Others Vs Muhammad Hussain through Legal Heirs & Others (PLD 1993 SC 147)]. Thus, the matter to the extent of transfer of land in question in favour of PTCL has attained the status of finality. 13. Further, the sale of land measuring 738-Kanals 06- Marlas including land measuring 13-Kanals & 10-Marlas Writ Petition No.45094/2021 9 Writ Petition No.62184/2021 Khasra Nos.229 & 230 of Mouza Dhana Singhwala by the Settlement Department in favour of respondent No.5 (PTCL) through T.O No.74635 dated 26.06.1979 which was given effect in Revenue Record through mutation No.771 dated 12.06.1982. The title document No.0000563 of the PTCL was duly verified by the Board of Revenue vide letter dated 10.07.1993. For ready reference, letter dated 10.07.1993 is reproduced as under: Phone No.353319 No. 116-93/PA/Secy (S&R) Board of Revenue, Punjab, Lahore, dated the 10th July, 1993 From: The Board of Revenue, Punjab. To The Addl: Deputy Commissioner (General) Lahore Cantt. Lahore Subject: VERIFICATION OF TRANSFER DOCUMENTS. Please refer to your memorandum No. ADC(G)/Cantt/323 Dated 28.2.1993 on the subject cited above. The record was examined by the Verification Committee in its meeting held on 5.7.1993 and observations made/orders passed by the Committee in this case are recorded in the minutes at Sr. No.1. Originally T.O.No.74635 was issued in favour of Pakistan Telegraph and Telephone Department through General Manager Lahore Telephone Region Lahore and 738 Kanals and 6 marlas of land as detailed in the said T.O at Page 4 situated in village Dhana Singhwala Tehsil Lahore was allotted. Subsequently revised T.O No.563 was issued in favour of T&T Department by virtue of which an area of 710 Kanals and 6 marlas of land as detailed at page No.4 of the said T.O. in village Dhana Singh Wala has been transferred.

Originally T.O.No.74635 was issued in favour of Pakistan Telegraph and Telephone Department through General Manager Lahore Telephone Region Lahore and 738 Kanals and 6 marlas of land as detailed in the said T.O at Page 4 situated in village Dhana Singhwala Tehsil Lahore was allotted. Subsequently revised T.O No.563 was issued in favour of T&T Department by virtue of which an area of 710 Kanals and 6 marlas of land as detailed at page No.4 of the said T.O. in village Dhana Singh Wala has been transferred. T.O No.563 found to have been issued in the name of T&T Department and is, therefore, verified. You are, therefore, requested to please take further action in the matter. Sd- (JAWAD AHMAD MUFTI) SECRETARY (SETT: & REH.) BOARD OF REVENUE, PUNJAB. Writ Petition No.45094/2021 10 Writ Petition No.62184/2021 No.116/93 P.A/Secy (S&R) A copy alongwith a verified copy of T.O No.563 in respect of land measuring 710 Kanals and 6 Marlas as detailed on page 4 of the said T.O situated in village Dhana Singh Wala in the name of T&T Department is forwarded to the Tehsildar, Lahore Cantt: Lahore for necessary action. However, before taking further action he shall re-confirm the validity of this letter on personal level from the undersigned. Sd- (JAWAD AHMAD MUFTI) SECRETARY (SETT: & REH.) BOARD OF REVENUE, PUNJAB. 14. From perusal of record of the writ petitions and Civil Revision Nos.2857 & 2858 of 1994 [wherein the record of civil suits is also available] it transpires that against the transfer of the land to T&T (PTCL) Department, Abdul Ghani son of Amar Ali filed suit [No.270/1/1990] for declaration with permanent injunction claiming to be owner of land measuring 07-Kanals falling Khasra No.230 Min. situated in Village Dhana Singhwala, Tehsil & District Lahore and also challenged mutation No.771 dated 12.06.1982 in favour of PTCL. In Suit No.270/1/1990 filed by Abdul Ghani, the memo of parties is as under:- “1. Province of Punjab through Secretary, Board of Revenue, Punjab, Lahore. 2. The Collector, Lahore District, Lahore. 3. The Federation of Pakistan through Secretary, Ministry of Communication, Government of Pakistan, Islamabad.” As per available record, the said suit was filed on 29.11.1990 and it was decreed ex-parte by the learned Senior Civil Judge, Lahore on 14.01.1991.

The proceedings Writ Petition No.45094/2021 11 Writ Petition No.62184/2021 conducted in the suit / complete order sheet are reproduced as under: " 29.11.90 وکلسن دمیع احرض دوعیٰ دجدی ےہ۔ درج ررٹسج وہوے۔ ونسٹ ابذخ ابلطہن رقتبر09.12.90 اجری وہ ں۔ ----- 09.12.90 وکلسن دمیع احرض اعرف ٹب راکیرڈ رپیک اجنمبن دماع ہیلع ربمن1 احرض۔ دماع مہیلع ربمن2 ،3 یک اجبن ےس وکیئ احرض ہن ےہ ۔ ان ےک الخف کی رطہف۔ رباےئ وجاب اجنمبن نمبر1 رقتبر 09.01.91 شیپ وہوے۔ ات اترخی وموجدہ وصرت ربرقار راھک اج ےئ ۔ یان ا ۔ای۔ ------ 09.1.91 وکلسن دمیع احرض ۔ امندنئہ دماع ہیلع ربمن1 احرض ۔ دماع مہیلع3 & 2 کے الخف کی رطہف۔ دماع ہیلع ربمن1 یک اجبن ےس اس اک امندنئہ ایبن ا دنبملق رک ان اچاتہ ےہ۔ ااکس ایبن دنبملق ایک اجات ےہ۔ ایبن امندنئہ دماع ہیلع ربمن1 ایبن ایک ہک دمیع ےک انم PTO ربمنی709071 ، ومرہخ12.3.92 ابتب ارایض دتموعہی اجری ایک ۔ای اھت۔ PTD 9404 21.03.99 ، انبم دمیع اجری ۔ای ۔ PTO/ PTD دوونں ذتمرکہ ابال ات اح خوسنخ ہن وہےئ ںی۔ دمیع ےک انم وکیئ اقب ا اجت ہن ںی اور امتم رمق ادا دش ہ ےہ۔ یسک مسق یکlitigation یھب زریِ امستع ہن ےہ۔ اس رطح دماع ہیلع ربمن1 دوعی ا وک ھذcontest ہن رکات ےہ۔ اور اس یک دح کت ڈرگی ایک اجےئ ۔ رکنس درتس میلست ایک۔" رباےئ وبثت کی رطہف دماع مہیلع3 & 2 یک دح کت رقتبر13.01.91 شیپ وہوے ۔ یان ا ۔ای۔ ------ 13.01.91 وکلسن دمیع احرض اجنمبن دماع مہیلع وکیئ احرض ہن ےہ۔ وبثت رطکیہف دنبملق دش۔ رباےئ ثحب و مکح رقتبر14.1.91 شیپ وہوے ۔ یان ا ۔ای۔ ------ 14.1.91 وکلسن دمیع ۔ احرض دوعیٰ وحباہل مکح رحبوف ارگنزیی ڈرگی ہعم رخہچ ایک اجات ےہ۔ لثم دعب ازرتبیت و لیمکت احمظف اخہن اجےئ ۔ یان ا ۔ای۔ 15. Perusal of the record of suits as well as above reproduced memos of parties shows that the Chief Settlement Commissioner / Administrator (Residual Properties) / Notified Officer, Board of Revenue, Punjab was not arrayed as party whereas Article 174 of the Constitution of Islamic Republic of Pakistan, 1973 as well as under Section 79, read with Order XXVII CPC the impleading of Province of Punjab through Chief Settlement Commissioner / Administrator (Residual Properties) / Notified Officer, Board of Revenue, was mandatory and without arraying it as a party to the lis, no effective decree could have been passed and even if a decree is passed in violation of the above, that would be inexecutable against the person who was not party to the said lis. The above proposition was conclusively settled in the earlier round of litigation which is reported as Province of the Punjab through Member Board of Revenue, (Residual Properties), Lahore & Others Vs Muhammad Writ Petition No.45094/2021 14 Writ Petition No.62184/2021 Hussain through Legal Heirs & Others (PLD 1993 SC 147) wherein the Hon‟ble Supreme Court of Pakistan has held that under Section 79 of the Civil Procedure Code read with Order XXVII, CPC and Article 174 of the Constitution, the suits against the Central Government be filed in the name of Pakistan (Federation) and against a Provincial Government in the name of the Province. For ready reference, Para-7 of the above said judgment is reproduced as under:- “7. If the trial Court, the appellate Court and the revisional Court had kept in view the requirements of section 79 of the Civil Procedure Code read with Order XXVII, C.P.C. they would not have entertained the claim at all with the parties arrayed in the form in which they were arrayed.

For ready reference, Para-7 of the above said judgment is reproduced as under:- “7. Section 79 of the Civil Procedure Code requires, and so does Article 174 of the Constitution, that all suits against the Central Government have to be filed in the name of Pakistan and against a Provincial Government in the name of the Province. Suing Pakistan, as was done in this case, through the Chief Settlement Commissioner and Member, Board of Revenue was in fact not only a mistake but a diversion of the proceedings materially affecting service and representation of the concerned defendants. The Chief Settlement Commissioner had ceased to be a legal entity or a functionary after repeal of the evacuee laws in 1974 - -much before the institution of the suit. Provincial Member, Board of Revenue could not represent Pakistan. Similar is the position of Deputy Custodian. There was no functionary of the description of Deputy Custodian after the repeal of the evacuee laws. As regards the Collector of the District, the procedure prescribed was quite a simple one --a straightforward one, for service on him. It is provided in Order XXVIII of the Civil Procedure Code that the Governments and their functionaries can be served through the Government Pleader. No effort, at any sage, was made to effect service through the Government Pleader, The Clerk of the Court of Chief Settlement Commissioner who made the appearance and even the lawyer appointed by the Solicitor was supposed to represent the Chief Settlement Commissioner when in fact no such office did exist in the eye of law. The revisional Court was in serious legal error in thinking and assuming that Collector represented ex- officio the province of Punjab and by impleading him the Province stood automatically impleaded.

Section 13 of the Displaced Persons Land Settlement Act, 1958 confers jurisdiction upon the Chief Settlement and Rehabilitation Commissioner to declare any evacuee land as a „building site‟. The above provision of law is reproduced as under: “13. Disposal of building sites. Any evacuee land declared by the Chief Settlement Commissioner to be a building site in pursuance of sub-clause (b) of clause of Section 2 shall be disposed of in accordance with the provisions of the Displaced Persons (Compensation and Rehabilitation Act, 1958 and.— (a) In case the land is situated within the limits of a Municipal Corporation, a Municipal Committee, a Sanitary Committee or a Cantonment, as such limits exist on the commencement of this Act, the sale proceeds thereof shall be credited to the compensation pool constituted under Section 4 of the Displaced Persons (Compensation and Rehabilitation) Act, 1958 (XXVIII of 1958); and (b) In other cases, the sale proceeds shall be credited to one or more of the compensation pools constituted under Section 5.” Thus, by exercising the power under the aforementioned provision of law, the Chief Settlement & Rehabilitation Commissioner, West Pakistan, Lahore issued a Notification No. 236-66/948-R(L) dated 24.02.1966 [published in the Gazette of West Pakistan dated 8th April, 1966] whereby Khasra Nos.229 & 230 of village Dhana Singhwala were declared as building site.

Moreover, Section 21 of the General Clauses Act, 1897 confers an inherent jurisdiction to an authority which has passed the order to reverse the erroneous or illegal order earlier passed by it. Similarly if any benefit has been obtained from authority by practicing misrepresentation or fraud, the same forum is vested with inbuilt jurisdiction to undo the wrong. In this regard the Hon‟ble Supreme Court of Pakistan in a case titled as Muhammad Baran and others Vs.
